summary.intent: Fix all new PR review comments on the `cstewart-hc/feat/deterministic-compaction` branch, specifically two Greptile-flagged issues around unused config fields and broken iterative re-compaction in structured compaction chains.
summary.outcome: Both P1 and P2 findings were fixed, tested (245 chat tests, 185 config tests, all green), committed, and pushed. Greptile was triggered for a second review pass.

repo: Greptile re-reviews are triggered manually via API; the CheckRun completing with '0 comments added' does not always mean no new findings — the issue comment body may be edited separately with a confidence-score summary containing new findings.

`extract_previous_summary` in structured.rs was incorrectly scoped to `history[..head_end]`, causing iterative structured→structured compaction chains to never detect a prior summary since the previous summary is appended at `head_end`, not within the head slice.

`summary_model` and `max_summary_tokens` in the compaction config are reserved/no-op fields for the structured strategy (tracked under moltis-8me); they exist in schema but are silently ignored without user warning.
`compute_auto_compact_threshold` helper introduced to wire `threshold_percent` into the auto-compact trigger; effective default changed from hardcoded 95% to 75% to match documented behavior.
Both `--default-features` and `--no-default-features` clippy passes are required for this crate.

code.finding: extract_previous_summary must scan full input history in reverse (not just head slice) to find the newest prior summary in structured→structured chains.

Added warn_if_unused_auxiliary_model_config helper that emits a runtime WARN when user sets summary_model or non-default max_summary_tokens, since these fields are not yet wired in the structured strategy.
Auto-compact trigger was previously hardcoded at 95%; now resolved dynamically via compute_auto_compact_threshold using the session persona's threshold_percent config.
summary_model and max_summary_tokens rustdoc updated to mark them as reserved pending moltis-8me implementation.
Config template comments for summary_model and max_summary_tokens updated to note reserved/no-op status.
Docs updated to explain that threshold_percent controls both the auto-compact trigger AND the tail-budget multiplier simultaneously — lowering it compacts earlier and shrinks the preserved tail.

workflow: Greptile's CheckRun result and its issue comment body are separate artifacts; a clean CheckRun does not guarantee no new findings in the summary comment.
Manually triggering a fresh Greptile review after significant fixes is necessary to get an updated confidence score and finding list.
Polling Greptile completion required multiple sequential bash poll commands rather than a single blocking wait.

friction: Greptile posted new P1/P2 findings by editing the original PR summary comment rather than posting inline review threads, making them easy to miss without explicitly fetching the full comment body.
Multiple sequential poll commands were needed to detect Greptile review completion — no built-in wait/webhook mechanism available.
Determining whether Greptile had truly finished required checking both the CheckRun status and the issue comment body separately, as they are not synchronized.
open_items: summary_model and max_summary_tokens are documented as reserved pending moltis-8me — full implementation deferred to that ticket.
Greptile round-2 review against the new commit was triggered but results not yet confirmed in this session.
The 'Picking a threshold' docs section now explains dual effects of threshold_percent, but operator tooling to surface the resolved threshold at runtime is limited to trace logs only.
